title: G2: Subtraction of M31 systemic 3D motion introduces correlated errors across satellites

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap.** The relative 3D velocity $\mathbf v_i = \mathbf v_{{\rm sat},i} - \mathbf v_{\rm M31}$ requires M31's own 3D motion (its proper motion, distance, and systemic LOS velocity). The uncertainty in $\mathbf v_{\rm M31}$ — especially M31's PM (van der Marel et al. 2019) — propagates into EVERY satellite's $v_{\perp,i}=\mathbf v_i\cdot\hat n$ in a *correlated* way.

**Why it matters.** A coherent error in $\mathbf v_{\rm M31}$ shifts all $v_{\perp,i}$ together and can MIMIC a coherent perpendicular signal (or mask one). Treating the satellites' errors as independent is therefore incorrect.

**Requirement.** (i) Write the frame transformation subtracting $\mathbf v_{\rm M31}$ in closed form. (ii) Identify the M31-systemic contribution to $v_{\perp,i}$ and show it is common-mode across $i$. (iii) Carry this term into the covariance matrix $\Sigma_{v_\perp}$ as off-diagonal correlations (it must NOT be diagonalized away). This is a prerequisite for the test in G5.
